episodes.title: Global FX: Payrolls prognosis, yen, GBP and HUF
episodes.summary:
**Summary**
JP Morgan's FX strategists dissect a weaker-than-expected July NFP print and its knock-on effects for the dollar, Fed pricing, and global carry trades — directly relevant given the Fed is the dominant driver of EM FX/rates positioning.

**Key points**
- Headline NFP missed; 3-month moving average payrolls growth revised down to 111K from 188K, closer to what economists see as true labor market strength (~100K/month).
- Unemployment rate unexpectedly dipped to 4.2%, but alongside a falling participation rate, so not an unambiguous positive; 4.2% would sit below the FOMC's 4.3% year-end forecast if sustained.
- Rates market pulled 5-6bp of July Fed cut pricing; December '26 pricing now below 30bp; dollar fell 0.8-1% versus most majors.
- House view remains bullish USD/bullish risk on sticky inflation forcing a later Fed hike debate, but house cautions against extrapolating post-NFP price action beyond a few days.
- Yen was the most active currency pre-NFP (breached 162), with markets on intervention watch; MOF has stayed quiet, possibly recalibrating tactics after limited effect of April/May intervention, with speculation of covert/ambush-style action or even US-Japan-Korea coordination (seen as low probability).
- Sterling: bullish call intact post-Reeves' fiscal discipline comments; EUR/GBP broke below 0.8620 and could extend toward 0.84 based on prior post-budget patterns.
- NOK: large Norges Bank FX purchase ($524mn) had muted market impact; EUR/NOK near fair value ~11.25, bullish NOK if dollar turns two-way.
- SEK: better PMI/tendency data supports building short SEK positions (GBP/SEK, USD/SEK) as market underpriced Swedish growth-to-carry regime shift.
- HUF: seen as bullish long-term convergence/appreciation story (ERM/euro adoption path) despite ~100bp of priced NBH cuts eroding carry; HUF real effective exchange rate still cheap vs CE3 peers.
- EM Asia/broad EM carry theme reinforced by Goldilocks NFP outcome — reduces fears of Fed hikes needed to defend EM currencies, supportive of high-yielders; Czech National Bank and Saudi (SAB) cited as hiking cycles anchoring carry.

**FX / rates implications**
- Softer NFP reduces near-term Fed hike risk, supportive of EM carry trades broadly (EM high-yielders outperformed post-print) — directly relevant to EM Asia and LATAM carry positioning even though not explicitly detailed for INR/IDR/MXN/BRL here.
- Dollar weakness (DXY -0.8-1%) is data-dependent, not a structural shift; desk maintains bullish USD bias into later-year Fed hike repricing, cautioning against chasing post-NFP dollar weakness.
- Yen intervention risk elevated into illiquid US holiday session; a soft-CPI-style spot hammering could open door to follow-through action.

**Watch list**
- Two more US employment reports before September FOMC needed for fuller labor market read.
- Japanese MOF verbal/actual intervention action over the next 24 hours, especially during the low-liquidity US holiday session.
- UK autumn budget/Labour Party conference for fiscal details affecting sterling.
- Hungary NBH rate cut delivery vs ~100bp priced, and HUF REER convergence trade.
